license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8dshs6e
title: DNA/RNA Radiolabeling Protocol
author: Liz O'Brien

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare G25 columns (from GE, green box): vortex thoroughly, twist cap ¼ turn, snap off bottom, spin for 00:01:00 at 3000 rpm to get rid of liquid.
Add 25 µL H 2 O to each labeling reaction after heat inactivation is done.
Since 50 μl H 2 O were in bottom of tube and you add your 50 μl reaction, you should end with up to 100 μl of 100 nanomolar (nM) labeled DNA/RNA.
Measure 1 µL of each reaction with the black rad counter on shelf to get cpm readings.
Heat inactivate the PNK at 65 °C for 00:20:00 .
Apply entire reaction (now 50 μl total) to G25 column resin.
Add 50 µL H 2 O to a labeled eppendorf tube, place G25 column in it.
Set up labeling reaction: %s %s Mix the DNA, buffer, enzyme, and H 2 O at the bench, and then add the DNA/enzyme mixture to ATP-filled tubes in a radioactive use area.
